name: Novotel Citygate Hong Kong Hotel
detail: location - excellent if you want to focus on trip to lantau island (big buddha), tai-o, citygate outlet mall, disneyland. very close to airport so you can target the last few days of stay in this hotel to prevent travelling back and forth to alternative hotels in hong kong island otherwise after your trip to lantau island. has a free shuttle bus every 15 minutes to hk airport. this hotel is linked to citygate outlet mall - you can travel to mtr via the mall. room - love the room. nice view of airport and mountains. ceiling to floor glass window. comfortable bed. gym - not bad. suitable for workout during trip. food - contains restaurant in the mall for ok price around 198 hkd for buffet, which is quite reasonable if you compare a similar buffet at other 4/5 star hotels in hong kong. of course it is expensive if you compare to food court etc. if you are looking for cheaper food, go to food republic at citygate outlet mall which is 10 minutes walk away inside the mall. nearby attractions - take bus 23 to see big buddha & poh lian monastery. take bus 1, 11, 21 to see guan yin monastery at lantau island. buy package ticket (55 hkd) if you want to go to tai-o & travel to all attractions in lantau island. it will allow you to travel in bus for a day around lantau. take mtr if you are heading to disneyland. shortcoming - price is a bit on the steep side. in fact more expensive than some hotels on tsim sha tsui of same star/facilities.
area: Hong Kong Airport,Hong Kong - View on map

name: Ibis Hong Kong Central & Sheung Wan Hotel
detail: Uhm...where do I even start? I travelled with a friend and upon arrival we could not get two double beds. That's not a bad thing at all. We were completely A-OKAY with this set up. When we went into our room though, we were ECSTATIC. Biggest double bed I have ever seen in Hong Kong. Fit the both of us and then some perfectly! Super comfortable. Fell asleep in bed each day like a baby. We stayed for six and the second deciding factor was definitely the floor. NO CARPET. LOVED THAT. The first was the bathroom. We did not like having a set up where the toilet and the shower were combined. But this bathroom was clean with a sliding door divide for the shower! I don't know how many hotels I have found that did not do that or were not as clean as this one! And the size of the room was PERFECT. It was a lot bigger than we imagined because space in Hong Kong is limited! There was even a mini fridge for you to use, which we totally did! Free WiFi made it all the more amazing as well. The lovely housekeeper and staff downstairs were also extraordinarily polite. Always held the doors open for us. I don't know about you guys, but I cannot recommend this place enough. It's also just a short walk to the Sheung Wan MTR. Complete convenience. But take into account that my friend and I love to walk. (Really, though, a five-minute walk is nothing.) Plus at night, it's totally quiet. We slept like babies. And despite getting a city view, it was still lovely opening up the drapes to massive windows. Our stay was amazing. My friend and I rave about our room many times during and after our stay. Thank you Ibis staff for making our trip back to Hong Kong THAT much more enjoyable. Much love! (The only con I can say is, it's not a very central location. If you plan on doing a lot of activities that are on Kowloon instead of on Hong Kong Island, it may be wise to go with another hotel. The MTR stops running at some point, folks. And taking a taxi all the time can cut into your budget!)
area: Hong Kong Central,Hong Kong - View on map

name: Regala Skycity Hotel by Regal Hotels
detail: Great place to stay overnight before flying back home.
area: Hong Kong Airport,Hong Kong - View on map

name: Regal Airport Hotel
detail: I was only there one day but could see that it is a top-class hotel. It has al the mod-cons in the room including a massive LED TV with a welcoming message for you and even speakers in the bathroom. Going to this hotel also really helped as I arrived at about 1am in Hong Kong and so didn't need to look for taxis or late-night transport and then hassle with check-ins in Hong Kong City. Most times you can only check in after 2pm anyway. And the good thing is it is not any more expensive than the normal (and usually much smaller) hotels in Hong Kong City.
area: Hong Kong Airport,Hong Kong - View on map

name: Atlas Guest House & Backpackers
detail: Excellent hotel and owner. I booked this hotel for my fiance while she was traveling alone and there was a misunderstanding with the booking and the owner was very good to work with. He gave me his personal WhatsApp number so he and I could work out all the details. He was very patient and my fiance had a great experience while staying. I would definitely book again while in Hong Kong.
area: Tsim Sha Tsui,Hong Kong - View on map

name: Ramada Hong Kong Harbour View
detail: I would say it is the Best Price ever if you would like to have a short stay in Hong Kong only for some good sleeping time every night. The location is fantastic. You can walk to Centre. The variety and food around the hotel are good and value for money. Hong Kong is small! You can not expect to have a large room like when you are doing a budget traveling in United States of America. Definitely worth it even though the space is small but, it is the best price ever in Hong Kong in a 4-star hotel. Good customer service! Excellent!
area: Hong Kong Island West,Hong Kong - View on map

name: Woody House
detail: Rooms are clean, hotel is safe and staff kind and attentive.

name: Burlington Hotel
detail: The Burlington was a pleasant surprise! Most hotels in Hong Kong are expensive and relatively small, and this was small, but it was great quality at a great price. It was very clean, comfortable, and modern. The staff were friendly, helpful, and very efficient! It’s in a great location on Hong Kong Island, and it was a really easy getting from the airport because the bus (A22) practically drops off right in front of the hotel! And it’s only like a 10-15 minute walk to the Hong Kong Convention Center. There are a ton of nice, cheap places to eat, a very convenient place to stay. I especially appreciated the complimentary Handy phone they include in every room, a cellphone with 4G that was super nice to have for navigation and communication while going around the city...much better than buying a sim and worrying about reception and running out of data. Overall, we were very pleased, and hope to stay at The Burlington again when we go back HK.

name: B P International
detail: the bp international hotel is the perfect hotel to those who wish to visit hongkong for shopping, sight seeing, food tripping, or simply just getting to know its cultural heritage. the hotel can be reached either via taxi cab or mtr from the airport. it's location is very strategic. every necessity could be obtained in a walking distance (money exchange, 7-eleven convenience store, street food, mtr station). it is also walking distance from the ever-famous canton road, for people who love to shop for designer items such as gucci, marc jacobs, lv, and many more. harbour city mall is also located near by. the austin mtr station is only 3 blocks away from the hotel. from there you can go to just about any point in hong kong as you wish! it is the cheapest mode of transport, and very easy to use. the hotel staff is very accommodating and can speak english very well. the hotel can also provide tourist concerns such as directions, tour guides, maps, and more. the rooms are small, but efficient for a stay in hong kong where you spend the whole day outside. it is perfect for relaxing after a long day of shopping and sight seeing. overall, i had a pleasant stay at the bp international hotel. im sure any one will also love this hotel! book now!
area: Tsim Sha Tsui,Hong Kong - View on map

name: Harbour Plaza North Point
detail: It was our first time in Hong Kong so we decided to get a 4 Star Hotel. After arriving at Hong Kong airport it took us a few minutes to find the Harbour Center Counter, but after we found it, we felt taken care of already. Cost us HKD$140 each for the airport shuttle to the Hotel, it was a fairly large bus, and it only seated a few of us. This is where we learned that the Airport Shuttle caters to other hotels too. Think of it this way, if you have large bags or luggage, a few bucks is worth it when going to and from the airport because you are going to walk a lot. My bag was a large duffle and it was 24 lbs. and I was so grateful I opted for the shuttle going to and from the Airport. Plus, you get to see Hong Kong more from the bus, unlike when you take the train which goes into the subway after the first 5-6 stations. When we arrived at the hotel, check in was a breeze, they just get the impression of my card and thats it, there was someone who helped us get our things from the bus to the check in counter then from the check-in counter to our room. But be sure to give a separate tip to the one who carries your bag from the bus to the hotel, because it will be a different person who will bring your bags up. The room was quite big/clean compared to other Hotels our friends stayed at. Their's were around 21 sq mts, ours was about 28 sq mts. they were complaining that they had traffic going to and from their bathrooms, while we were comfortable in a King sized bed, we had a stocked mini ref and we had cable. Only problem is internet costed around a HK$100/day. We only stayed at the hotel to sleep and bathe mostly, but we would've loved to use the gym and the pool. Both were clean and complete. Maybe because there are monthly tenants staying at the serviced suites of the hotel. They had a sauna but I didn't get to use it. My girlfriend was able to use the pool, her only complaint was that it wasn't heated. We are coming back, soon.
area: Quarry Bay,Hong Kong - View on map
